Project.NAME: ocserv-admin-mcp
Project.keywords: ocserv, openconnect, anyconnect, mcp, vpn-admin, access-control, policy-groups, infrastructure-automation, audit-logging, agent-integration
Project.annotation: A generic safe administrative package for `ocserv` that lets an operator manage VPN users, policy groups, and active sessions without manually editing server configuration. It exposes a constrained MCP tool surface and localhost backend for safe execution on the VPS, and can be integrated into Nanobot or any other MCP-capable agent.

DA-USER.meaning: User is the managed VPN identity.

DA-POLICY-GROUP.meaning: Group is an ocserv policy group, not a separate L3 network.

DA-NETWORK-GROUP.meaning: Network-group wording in MVP refers to a set of access rules or routes rather than an overlay VPN construct.

DA-SESSION.meaning: Session is active occtl-visible runtime connection state for a VPN identity.

DA-COMMAND-AUDIT-ENTRY.meaning: CommandAuditEntry is the structured audit record for operator intent, execution, and outcome.

M-BACKEND-CLIENT.NAME: backend_client
M-BACKEND-CLIENT.purpose: Validate approved action requests and translate them into constrained localhost backend calls without performing administrative work directly.

fn-planAction.PURPOSE: Validate an approved admin action request

fn-requireConfirmation.PURPOSE: Escalate dangerous actions into an explicit confirmation flow

type-MvpCommand.PURPOSE: Canonical public tool vocabulary locked in Phase 0

M-MCP-SERVER.NAME: mcp_server
M-MCP-SERVER.purpose: Expose the approved admin tool vocabulary through a local MCP stdio server that routes only to the localhost backend.

fn-handle_message.PURPOSE: Serve initialize, tools/list, and tools/call requests for the strict public admin tool set

fn-buildServerFromEnv.PURPOSE: Build the MCP server from environment-configured backend settings

M-TOOL-POLICY.NAME: tool_policy
M-TOOL-POLICY.purpose: Provide workspace instructions that force ocserv administration through the approved tool surface instead of shell or direct config edits.

fn-policy.PURPOSE: Constrain MCP clients to approved backend tools and response handling rules

M-OCSERV-ADMIN-API.NAME: ocserv_admin_api
M-OCSERV-ADMIN-API.purpose: Expose a constrained localhost HTTP backend that implements the whitelist of allowed administrative operations and only accepts authenticated loopback clients.
M-OCSERV-ADMIN-API.depends: M-SAFETY-CONTROLS, M-USER-LIFECYCLE, M-POLICY-GROUP-MANAGER, M-SESSION-MANAGER, M-OCSERV-ADAPTER, M-AUDIT-LOG

fn-executeApprovedAction.PURPOSE: Run a single approved admin action under policy constraints and return structured activation evidence

fn-validateRequest.PURPOSE: Validate API payloads, including force-delete preconditions

fn-build_app.PURPOSE: Construct the localhost WSGI application callable

fn-serve.PURPOSE: Run the localhost admin API server

M-OCSERV-ADAPTER.NAME: ocserv_adapter
M-OCSERV-ADAPTER.purpose: Wrap ocserv system operations, deterministic managed config files, validation, reload handling, rollback, and command execution safely.

fn-inventoryConfig.PURPOSE: Inventory managed config, auth, group templates, and user-group mapping surfaces

fn-preflightMutation.PURPOSE: Validate mutation preconditions and deterministic write scope before edits

fn-applyManagedMutation.PURPOSE: Apply mutate -> validate -> reload -> verify with rollback on failure

fn-runOcctl.PURPOSE: Execute occtl commands and normalize outputs

fn-validateConfig.PURPOSE: Validate ocserv-related config before service mutation

fn-reloadService.PURPOSE: Reload ocserv safely with rollback-oriented safeguards

M-USER-LIFECYCLE.NAME: user_lifecycle_manager
M-USER-LIFECYCLE.purpose: Own VPN identity creation, disablement, removal, and existence checks against the chosen ocserv auth backend.

fn-createUser.PURPOSE: Create a VPN identity through the approved auth backend path with deterministic activation evidence

fn-disableUser.PURPOSE: Disable a VPN identity without silently deleting it

fn-removeUser.PURPOSE: Delete a VPN identity through the approved lifecycle path with active-session force protection

fn-listUsers.PURPOSE: List managed VPN identities from the canonical user store

M-POLICY-GROUP-MANAGER.NAME: policy_group_manager
M-POLICY-GROUP-MANAGER.purpose: Assign users to ocserv policy groups and render policy-related configuration changes safely.

fn-assignGroup.PURPOSE: Assign a user to the intended ocserv policy group through the deterministic adapter pipeline

fn-renderPolicyChanges.PURPOSE: Describe scoped policy rendering without corrupting unrelated files

M-SESSION-MANAGER.NAME: session_manager
M-SESSION-MANAGER.purpose: List active sessions and disconnect active sessions through verified ocserv control surfaces.

fn-listSessions.PURPOSE: Retrieve real active session state from occtl

fn-disconnectSessionForUser.PURPOSE: Disconnect an active ocserv session for one VPN identity

M-AUDIT-LOG.NAME: audit_log
M-AUDIT-LOG.purpose: Emit an immutable-ish operation trail with structured redaction-aware fields for administrative actions.

fn-recordAuditEvent.PURPOSE: Record action intent, outcome, and safe evidence fields

M-SAFETY-CONTROLS.NAME: safety_controls
M-SAFETY-CONTROLS.purpose: Enforce confirmations, allowlists, dangerous-action guards, and execution invariants before system mutation.

fn-guardAction.PURPOSE: Reject unsafe or out-of-scope actions before execution

fn-confirmDestructiveAction.PURPOSE: Require explicit confirmation for destructive operations

M-CONFIRMATION-STATE.NAME: confirmation_state
M-CONFIRMATION-STATE.purpose: Track pending destructive actions, explicit confirmations, expiry, and replay-safe state transitions.

fn-createPendingConfirmation.PURPOSE: Create a pending destructive-action confirmation record

fn-resolvePendingConfirmation.PURPOSE: Resolve a confirmation as confirmed, cancelled, expired, or replayed

CrossLink.relation: uses strict action planning and response normalization from
routes approved tool calls to over bearer-authenticated loopback HTTP
forces clients to use the approved MCP tool surface exposed by
dispatches approved actions to over bearer-authenticated loopback HTTP
consults confirmation and guard policy in
delegates user create, disable, and remove operations to
executes auth-backend and deterministic config-safe lifecycle changes through
requires identity and destructive-action policy from
applies ocserv policy changes through deterministic managed-file mutations
records rejected and confirmed actions with
